summary.scholar: On January 31, 1865, the House approved the Thirteenth Amendment abolishing slavery throughout the entire nation, going beyond the Emancipation Proclamation.
summary.explorer: On January 31, 1865, Congress voted to add the Thirteenth Amendment to the Constitution, making slavery illegal everywhere in America.

significance.scholar: The Thirteenth Amendment permanently ended slavery in the United States. It was ratified December 6, 1865.
significance.explorer: This was one of the most important changes ever made to the Constitution. No person in America could ever be enslaved again.
